summary: Frontend-only verification of ForkFate sponsor plan toggle, admin on-demand sponsor summary email, and install nudge behavior. All required flows work as specified.

issues: Both Monthly and Yearly cards show identical ring/border classes when selected (visual selection indicator relies on same class regardless of which is active). The 'selected' state is applied to whichever is clicked, but both cards had matching 'border-[#E01E26] ring-1 ring-[#E01E26]' classes at inspection time — verify only the active plan should carry the ring styles. Note text updates correctly, so functional selection works; only the highlight class parity is worth a quick visual double-check.

critical_code_review_comments: Console error observed on /admin: CORS-blocked fetch to https://ipapi.co/latitude/. This third-party geolocation call fails silently but pollutes the console. Consider removing or guarding against the CORS failure on the admin route where it is not needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sponsor dialog: header-sponsor-link opens sponsor-dialog. Toggle Monthly ($29/mo, First month FREE) / Yearly ($290/yr, 2 months free, Save $58/yr badge) both present. Note text switches: 'Free first month, then $29/month · cancel anytime' vs 'Billed $290 today, then annually · cancel anytime'. Subscribe button present (PayPal LIVE — not clicked). Admin /admin login with GrimReaper!2026 succeeds, Security & cost card shows sponsor-summary-email section with 'Send summary now' button. Click returns success toast 'Sponsor summary email sent'. Stat cards MRR/Paying Subs/Active Spots/Engagement render without errors. Install nudge not verified (requires PWA beforeinstallprompt, not fireable in headless) — per request, this is non-blocking.

observations.sponsor_dialog: PASS - toggle, prices, savings badge, note switching all correct
observations.admin_summary_email: PASS - button present, click shows 'Sponsor summary email sent' toast
observations.install_nudge: SKIPPED - PWA beforeinstallprompt unavailable in headless (non-blocking per request)
